mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.18% as of May 2009), sustained near-zero policy rates compress equity discount rates and encourage capital reallocation from cash/bonds to risk assets, producing positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month as long as the rate stays at the floor.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% May 2009, near record low
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during zero-rate periods are not statistically higher than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month increase in UNRATE decelerates relative to the prior month's increase, it signals that labor market deterioration is peaking, which historically precedes short-horizon equity rallies as markets price in an approaching cyclical trough within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE monthly increase is smaller than the prior month's increase (deceleration in rate of rise)
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose to 9.4% May 2009 from 8.9% April 2009
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following deceleration signals are negative or indistinguishable from baseline in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint stabilization of INDPRO (smaller month-over-month declines) and PAYEMS (decelerating job losses) reflects a coincident bottoming of the business cycle; because neither series alone reliably signals a trough, only their simultaneous deceleration produces a reliable positive S&P 500 signal over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS month-over-month declines are smaller in magnitude than their respective 3-month trailing average declines
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 85.9344 April 2009 showing steep decline
fact:PAYEMS revised 131466 thousand, continued job losses but decelerating pace
regime_hint: recession bottoming with coincident indicator confirmation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint deceleration signals underperform unconditional 60-day returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone (interaction coefficient not significant at 10% level).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from historically depressed levels (458-478 thousand units, near multi-decade lows) signals the early stage of a housing-led demand recovery, which is priced into equities with a lag, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically during the post-housing-crash recovery regime.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its trailing 6-month average after having been below it for at least 4 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 478 thousand April 2009, near historic trough
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after HOUST trough-rebound signals are not significantly positive versus baseline (p > 0.05) in an out-of-sample test spanning 2009-2012, or the effect reverses sign outside the post-shock regime.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent narrowing trend in the monthly change of PAYEMS (i.e., job losses shrinking month over month) reflects improving labor market momentum that forward-predicts positive equity returns over the following month, functioning as a continuous always-on trend signal regardless of the absolute employment level.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 131466 thousand May 2009, down from 132151 thousand vintage estimate
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly-change trend and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the relationship inverts sign persistently for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses below the 0.25% effective zero-bound threshold, conventional monetary policy space is exhausted, forcing markets to anticipate unconventional easing (quantitative easing), which is interpreted as a strong liquidity signal that lifts equity prices over the subsequent month until the threshold is breached upward.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / unconventional easing regime
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns while FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% are not significantly different from returns when FEDFUNDS > 0.25% in matched-period comparison, or effect disappears once FEDFUNDS rises above threshold in later regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stabilization of RSAFS (retail sales flattening or rising month-over-month) and HOUST (housing starts ticking up) jointly signal an inflection in aggregate consumer and residential demand; because either series alone is noisy at high frequency, only their joint co-movement produces a reliable short-horizon positive equity reaction within one trading week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change turns non-negative and HOUST month-over-month change turns positive in the same reporting month
regime_hint: consumer demand inflection during recession trough
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following joint RSAFS/HOUST stabilization signals show no significant difference from unconditional 5-day returns, or single-series models achieve equal or better predictive power, negating the interaction premise.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recession trough, a narrowing in the 3-month annualized rate of INDPRO decline (relative to the prior 3-month period) signals that the industrial contraction is bottoming, which forward-predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter specifically within deep manufacturing downturn regimes.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized INDPRO decline is smaller in magnitude than the prior 3-month annualized decline
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 85.9344 April 2009, sharply below 2017=100 base, deep contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration signals are not significantly positive relative to baseline (p > 0.05) when tested across the 2001 and 2008-09 recession troughs, or the signal fails to replicate out-of-sample in subsequent industrial recessions.
